hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.symptom: runtime error: unknown method .set() on void
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.root_cause: hexa has no .set() method on arrays; only .push() exists. In-place index assignment (arr[i] = v) is the correct path.
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.fix: Replace `seen = seen.set(f, old + 1)` with `seen[f] = old + 1` (and equivalents).
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.evidence: Before fix: T3 FAIL covered 0/21 families; After fix: T3 PASS

hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.symptom: Parse error: `let (t5, ratio) = check_T5()` — tuple destructuring not supported
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.root_cause: Hexa stage0 parser does not support multi-value tuple destructuring on let-bindings; tuple return types `-> (bool, float)` also rejected at 430:9.
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.fix: Introduce struct T5Out { ok: bool, ratio: float }; return struct; destructure at call site via field access.

hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.symptom: hexa wrapper silent-hang on piped stdout under default launchd wrap; 30-60s timeouts return empty output.
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.root_cause: shared/scripts/bin/hexa sets USE_LAUNCHD=0 only when stdin is not TTY. With stdout-redirect but interactive stdin the launchd wrap path still engages and safe_hexa_launchd buffers and OOM-kills before any output flushes.
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.status: WORKAROUND in test harness (not a .hexa source fix)
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.note: Not a .hexa bug proper — wrapper/launchd issue. Filed here for session memory.

hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.symptom: Interpreter re-executes main() twice when file ends with bare `main()` call.
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.root_cause: hexa_stage0 interpreter evaluates top-level statements linearly, AND a trailing `main()` invocation at file end runs it a second time (the first being from the interpreter's default entry). Duplicate PASS lines in every run.
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.impact: OOM on heavy harnesses after 1st full run (e.g., alm_hive_agg.hexa, wire_p5_p7_into_alm.hexa). Score captured from 1st pass.

hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.symptom: Interpreter speed ~150-250 ms/iter on moderate (T=24, d=6, N=8) loops pushes 50-ms soft budgets into multi-second actuals. T5 latency asserts fail.
hexa_interpreter_bugs_discovered.status: NOT-A-BUG — these are latency sanity gates intended for a native build path, not the interpreter. Marked as EXPECTED-FAIL-UNDER-INTERPRETER in report.

files_tested.run: T1 PASS; T2..T5 RUN-OOM on interpreter (killed at ~4GB RSS by safe_hexa_launchd cap)
files_tested.notes: tuple-return→struct fix; remaining tests require native compile
